articles.title: The RAM shortage could last years
articles.summary: RAM shortage is expected to persist until 2027-2030, with suppliers meeting only 60% of demand by 2027 despite increased DRAM production efforts.

articles.title: OpenAI’s former Sora boss is leaving
articles.summary: Bill Peebles, leader of OpenAI's Sora video generation team, is leaving the company after OpenAI discontinued the project. The departure reflects OpenAI's strategic shift to focus on coding and enterprise products.

articles.title: Should you stare into Sam Altman’s orb before your next date?
articles.summary: Tinder partners with World's identity-verifying orbs to let users prove they're human and earn free boosts. Sam Altman's company expands the biometric verification service to select markets including Japan and the US.

articles.title: Tesla brings its robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston
articles.summary: Tesla expanded its driverless robotaxi service to Dallas and Houston, joining Austin as the third Texas city. The service now operates without safety drivers following its January 2026 launch.

articles.title: AI chip startup Cerebras files for IPO
articles.summary: Cerebras, an AI chip startup, files for IPO following major deals with AWS and OpenAI worth over $10 billion to supply chips for data centers.

articles.title: Anthropic’s relationship with the Trump administration seems to be thawing
articles.summary: Despite Pentagon's supply-chain risk designation, Anthropic maintains dialogue with Trump administration officials, suggesting improving relations.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a San Francisco cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million Series B to build AI-native infrastructure competing with AWS and Google Cloud.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code costs $20-$200/month for AI-powered coding, but free alternative Goose offers similar functionality as open-source software running locally without subscription fees or rate limits.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard campaign using AI tokens and coding challenges to recruit engineers. The creative hiring stunt decoded into a Berghain nightclub algorithm puzzle that attracted thousands of applicants.

articles.title: US-sanctioned currency exchange says $15 million heist done by "unfriendly states"
articles.summary: Kyrgyzstan-registered cryptocurrency exchange Grinex halted operations after a $15 million heist, claiming state-sponsored attackers were responsible. Researchers confirmed the theft targeting Russian users.

articles.title: Recent advances push Big Tech closer to the Q-Day danger zone
articles.summary: Recent advances in quantum computing bring potential threats closer to Q-Day, when quantum machines could break current encryption. The 2012 Flame malware incident demonstrates vulnerabilities in cryptographic systems that could be exploited at scale.

articles.title: “Negative” views of Broadcom driving thousands of VMware migrations, rival says
articles.summary: Broadcom's VMware takeover has driven customer dissatisfaction, with rival Nutanix claiming to have attracted thousands of VMware customers. Nutanix CEO stated the company now has about 30,000 customers, many migrating from VMware.

articles.title: Pie Day 2026
articles.summary: Ellie organized a Pi Day 2026 event at MIT's Food Institute, orchestrating the baking of 30 pies. The posts document both the celebration and behind-the-scenes preparation of this large-scale baking project.

articles.title: The Download: bad news for inner Neanderthals, and AI warfare’s human illusion
articles.summary: French geneticists challenge the popular theory that modern humans carry Neanderthal DNA from ancient interbreeding, proposing population structure as an alternative explanation for genetic similarities.

articles.title: The case for fixing everything
articles.summary: Stewart Brand's new book 'Maintenance: Of Everything, Part One' argues that upkeep and repair of systems is civilizationally important and a radical act deserving honor for maintainers.

articles.title: Building a Fast Multilingual OCR Model with Synthetic Data
articles.summary: Researchers developed a fast multilingual OCR model using synthetic data to recognize text across multiple languages efficiently, reducing the need for expensive manual annotations.

articles.title: Ecom-RLVE: Adaptive Verifiable Environments for E-Commerce Conversational Agents
articles.summary: Researchers introduce Ecom-RLVE, an adaptive verifiable environment for training e-commerce conversational agents using reinforcement learning. The system enables agents to learn realistic shopping interactions with built-in verification mechanisms.

articles.title: The PR you would have opened yourself
articles.summary: Article discusses how AI can automatically generate pull requests developers would create themselves, streamlining development workflows and reducing manual coding tasks through intelligent automation.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future of artificial intelligence and emerging trends in AI development. The conversation explores next steps in advancing AI technology and its implications.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creative capabilities, streamlining content creation and business operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft open sources its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural AI technology publicly available to advance sustainable farming practices and innovation.

articles.title: 7 ways to travel smarter this summer, with help from Google
articles.summary: Google's latest tools help travelers plan trips, find deals, and explore destinations more efficiently. The article provides seven tips for smarter summer travel using Google's features.

articles.title: A new way to explore the web with AI Mode in Chrome
articles.summary: Chrome introduces AI Mode upgrades that transform web interaction. Users can now explore the web more effectively with enhanced AI capabilities integrated directly into the browser.

articles.title: New ways to create personalized images in the Gemini app
articles.summary: Nano Banana 2 now generates personalized images using your Google Photos and personal context, creating visuals that reflect your unique life and experiences.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS: the next generation of expressive AI speech
articles.summary: Gemini 3.1 Flash TTS introduces granular audio tags enabling precise control over AI speech generation for more expressive audio output.

articles.title: Gemini Robotics-ER 1.6: Powering real-world robotics tasks through enhanced embodied reasoning
articles.summary: Gemini Robotics ER 1.6 enhances autonomous robotics through improved spatial reasoning and multi-view understanding, enabling robots to better perform real-world tasks.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, setting new standards for intelligent AI systems.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family featuring mixture-of-experts architecture.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, using generative AI to enhance video game graphics in real-time up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting conditions to generate photorealistic details.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 covers major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR pricing, Cursor's coding automations, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to automatically identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing debugging challenges in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses challenges in complex indoor environments by improving target localization, self-localization, and path planning capabilities.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distilling LLMs for faster reasoning reduces their ability to explore alternative solutions on complex problems, revealing a fundamental tradeoff between speed and generalization performance.

articles.title: Why harness engineering is becoming the new AI moat
articles.summary: As large language models become commoditized, sophisticated engineering systems around them increasingly provide competitive advantage. Anthropic's Claude Code leak highlights how harness engineering is becoming the true differentiator.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: xAI Launches Standalone Grok Speech-to-Text and Text-to-Speech APIs, Targeting Enterprise Voice Developers
articles.summary: xAI launched standalone Grok Speech-to-Text and Text-to-Speech APIs for enterprise developers, competing with ElevenLabs and Deepgram. The APIs leverage infrastructure from Grok Voice across mobile, Tesla, and Starlink platforms.

articles.title: A Coding Tutorial for Running PrismML Bonsai 1-Bit LLM on CUDA with GGUF, Benchmarking, Chat, JSON, and RAG
articles.summary: Tutorial on running Bonsai 1-bit LLM with CUDA and GGUF, covering setup, inference optimization, quantization techniques, and real-world applications including chat, JSON generation, and RAG workflows.

articles.title: A Coding Guide for Property-Based Testing Using Hypothesis with Stateful, Differential, and Metamorphic Test Design
articles.summary: Tutorial on property-based testing using Hypothesis framework, demonstrating stateful, differential, and metamorphic testing approaches to automatically generate test cases and uncover bugs beyond traditional unit testing.

articles.title: After testing this HP laptop, I get why its 'boring' design is adored by business users
articles.summary: HP's ZBook 8 G1i prioritizes traditional business workstation performance over sleek design and battery life, explaining why professionals favor its practical approach.

articles.title: The best TV antenna of 2026: Expert tested
articles.summary: TV antennas provide free access to local news, sports, and entertainment channels, eliminating expensive cable subscriptions. Expert-tested options for 2026 offer reliable indoor and outdoor reception.

articles.title: Your old iPad or Android tablet can be your new smart home panel - here's how
articles.summary: Old tablets can be repurposed as smart home control panels. Learn how to transform a forgotten iPad or Android device into a functional smart home hub.

articles.title: The Complete Guide to Inference Caching in LLMs
articles.summary: Inference caching in LLMs reduces costs and latency when calling APIs at scale by storing and reusing previous computations for repeated inputs.

articles.title: Python Decorators for Production Machine Learning Engineering
articles.summary: Article discusses using Python decorators for production machine learning engineering, helping developers write cleaner, more efficient ML code.

articles.title: 5 Techniques for Efficient Long-Context RAG
articles.summary: Article presents five techniques for optimizing Retrieval-Augmented Generation systems handling long contexts, improving efficiency and performance.

articles.title: Your RAG System Retrieves the Right Data — But Still Produces Wrong Answers. Here’s Why (and How to Fix It).
articles.summary: RAG systems can retrieve correct documents but produce wrong answers when conflicting information exists in the same retrieval window. A simple pipeline fix resolves this issue without additional models or resources.

articles.title: AI Agents Need Their Own Desk, and Git Worktrees Give Them One
articles.summary: Git worktrees enable AI agents to work in parallel coding sessions with isolated environments, reducing setup overhead and improving agent efficiency.

articles.title: How to Learn Python for Data Science Fast in 2026 (Without Wasting Time)
articles.summary: Learn efficient Python strategies for data science without wasting time, based on practical experience and proven methods for rapid skill development.

articles.title: No Need for Space Gear — Capcom’s ‘PRAGMATA’ Joins GeForce NOW on Launch Day
articles.summary: Capcom's PRAGMATA sci-fi action game launches on GeForce NOW simultaneously with its worldwide release, allowing cloud streaming to any device. Five additional titles join the platform this week.

articles.title: Rethinking AI TCO: Why Cost per Token Is the Only Metric That Matters
articles.summary: Data centers have transformed into AI token factories, making cost per token the primary economic metric. Traditional TCO assessments focusing on chip specs and FLOPS per dollar are now obsolete for evaluating AI infrastructure.

articles.title: New Adobe Premiere Color Grading Mode Accelerated on NVIDIA GPUs
articles.summary: Adobe Premiere introduces a new Color Grading Mode in beta, accelerated by NVIDIA RTX technology. The feature was showcased at NAB Show 2026, demonstrating enhanced creative workflows for professional video editors.

articles.title: Introducing granular cost attribution for Amazon Bedrock
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock now offers granular cost attribution for AI inference, automatically tracking costs by IAM principal and enabling detailed chargeback and optimization across teams and projects.

articles.title: Optimize video semantic search intent with Amazon Nova Model Distillation on Amazon Bedrock
articles.summary: Amazon Nova model distillation on Bedrock optimizes video semantic search by balancing accuracy and latency. The approach reduces latency from larger models while maintaining search intent routing intelligence.

articles.title: Power video semantic search with Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings
articles.summary: Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings enable video semantic search, allowing organizations to quickly find specific moments in video content for sports highlights, archived footage, and news retrieval to enhance user experiences and monetization.

articles.title: Full-Stack Optimizations for Agentic Inference with NVIDIA Dynamo
articles.summary: NVIDIA Dynamo enables full-stack optimizations for agentic inference, with coding agents now generating production code at scale across companies like Stripe, Ramp, and Spotify.

articles.title: Build a More Secure, Always-On Local AI Agent with OpenClaw and NVIDIA NemoClaw
articles.summary: OpenClaw and NVIDIA NemoClaw enable secure, always-on local AI agents that perform autonomous tasks like file reading, API calls, and multi-step workflows beyond simple Q&A.

articles.title: Accelerate Clean, Modular, Nuclear Reactor Design with AI Physics
articles.summary: AI accelerates development of Small Modular Reactors (SMRs) and Generation IV nuclear designs to create safer, cleaner, and more economical nuclear energy solutions through physics-based optimization.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive and interact with social media advertisements across various platforms, revealing generational attitudes toward ads.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing carbon footprint mitigation technology and sharing it openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: A person with a non-traditional background developed innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences drive breakthrough advancements in XR.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to understand how Large Language Models make decisions through feature attribution analysis, advancing AI interpretability and safety at scale.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven design of imaging systems that uses an encoder to map objects to images and quantifies measurement quality through noise models and information estimation.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm using divide-and-conquer paradigm instead of temporal difference learning, offering better scalability for long-horizon tasks.
